text: Press Ctrl+Enter (or Cmd+Enter on Mac) to run the selected query, or the entire script if nothing is selected. This is the fastest way to execute without reaching for the mouse.
Open the ERD tab in Explorer to visualize your database schema as an interactive entity-relationship diagram. You can pan, zoom, and click tables to inspect their columns and relationships.
Use the AI assistant in Explorer to describe what you want in plain language and get a SQL query generated for you. Look for the AI panel on the right side of the query editor.

Assign environments like Production, QA, or Development to your connections. Connections tagged as Critical show a distinct color in the interface, helping you avoid running risky queries on the wrong database.
You can have multiple database connections open at the same time. Switch between them in the Explorer panel — each gets its own query tabs and results.
Enable Interactive Tables in Settings → Results View Config to unlock row creation, deletion, and in-cell editing directly in the results grid.
Turn on key icons in Settings → Results View Config to display primary key and foreign key indicators next to column headers when browsing table data.
The Profiler captures live query activity on your SQL Server connections. Use it to find slow queries, track execution counts, and diagnose performance issues in real time.
Use the Compare feature to diff two databases side by side — schema structure, table contents, indexes, and more. Great for spotting drift between environments.
Switch between Neon Dark, Solar Light, and System Sync themes in Settings → Appearance. System Sync follows your OS dark/light mode automatically.
Adjust Font Scaling in Settings → Appearance to make the interface larger or smaller. This scales the entire UI — useful on high-DPI displays or when you want a more comfortable reading size.
Syntax highlighting in the query editor can be toggled in Settings → Appearance. Disabling it may improve performance when working with very large scripts.
Create custom environments in Settings → General to label and color-code your connections. Set an environment as Critical to enable extra confirmation prompts before executing destructive queries.
Set a query timeout in Settings → Databases Config to automatically cancel queries that run too long. Set it to 0 for no timeout — useful for long-running migrations.
The Docs section contains the full built-in documentation for Spiral, including feature guides, keyboard shortcuts, and tips for getting the most out of the application.
Turn off Show Toolbar Text Buttons in Settings → Appearance to display icons only in the Explorer toolbar. This frees up horizontal space, giving your query tabs more room.
Press Ctrl+Shift+T (or Cmd+Shift+T on Mac) to instantly capture a screenshot of the window at its current size and jump straight to the save dialog — no need to open the Take Screenshot dialog first.
